bugs_found_and_fixed.bug: Mass-assigned kur (DEDR 1638) to 205 LOW signs via Elamite corroboration
bugs_found_and_fixed.root_cause: Phase-111 mass-assigned kur reading; Phase-239 injected same DEDR for all; Phase-271 upgraded to HIGH
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: Phase 309: Reverted 205 bogus kur, kept 20 legitimate (allograph-based)

bugs_found_and_fixed.bug: Mass-assigned kol (DEDR 2133) to 205 reverted signs via positional re-derivation
bugs_found_and_fixed.root_cause: used_dedr counter only tracked HIGH signs, not newly-assigned ones, so kol scored highest for every sign
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: Audit revert: all 205 Phase-312 signs back to LOW with no reading

bugs_found_and_fixed.bug: Venkatesan cross-check reported 0% agreement due to Unicode diacritical mismatch
bugs_found_and_fixed.root_cause: String comparison failed on ō vs o, ṭ vs t, etc. in Dravidian transcription
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: Corrected with unicodedata normalization: actual agreement is 5% (1 exact + 2 close out of 55)
bugs_found_and_fixed.status: FIXED in this file; output needs re-generation

claims_retracted.reason: Phase 317 permutation null test showed random shuffled readings produce 94.2% conformance (HIGHER than real). The PD transition rules are too permissive to discriminate.
Phase 312 mass-assigned kol to 205 signs. After revert, only 400 signs have readings.
Relied on Phase 312 mass-assigned kol readings. Actual HIGH-only coverage is 92.8%.

experiments_contaminated_by_bugs.Phase 308 Elamite: Ran before Phase 309 kur fix. However, the Elamite LM construction and Holdat SA are anchor-independent. The anchored bigram test used 605 anchors (including 205 bogus kur) — bigram hit rates may be slightly inflated but the RANKING (Dravidian > Elamite > Munda > Hebrew > Uniform) is robust.
experiments_contaminated_by_bugs.Phase 313 Grammar: Used kol-contaminated anchors AND was independently invalidated by Phase 317. RETRACTED.
experiments_contaminated_by_bugs.Phase 314 Formulas: Used kol-contaminated anchors for MEDIUM/LOW signs. HIGH-only formula patterns (PROFESSION+SUFFIX) are unaffected. The '1252 fully decoded' count was inflated.
experiments_contaminated_by_bugs.Phase 317 Permutation: Used kol-contaminated anchors but conclusion (NOT SIGNIFICANT) is CORRECT regardless — the test is valid because it shows the grammar rules don't discriminate.
experiments_contaminated_by_bugs.Phase 321 Venkatesan: Comparison code had diacritical bug. Corrected rate: 5% (not 0%).

experiments_verified_clean.Phase 310 M77: Used corrected 400H anchors. Dravidian 70.5% vs Uniform 0%. VALID.
experiments_verified_clean.Phase 311 Phon: Used corrected anchors. 76% PD inventory coverage. VALID.
experiments_verified_clean.Phase 315 Scorecard: Sign-level metrics, does not use anchor readings. VALID.
experiments_verified_clean.Phase 319 Entropy: Used HIGH-only reading sequences. H2=4.11 bits. VALID.
experiments_verified_clean.Phase 321b Kriger: Uniqueness test is sign-level, independent of anchors. 97.7%. VALID.

valid_findings: 5-way language discrimination: Dravidian best fit (anchored bigram test)
50% Parpola agreement (8 exact + 2 partial out of 20)
5% Venkatesan agreement (1 exact + 2 close out of 55)
76% PD phonological inventory attested (4/6 missing are expected rare)
invalid_findings_retracted: 91.8% PD grammar conformance (permutation null showed non-discriminative)
605 signs with readings (was kol mass-assignment; actual: 400)
